display_name: Electric Power and Heating Effect (Joule's Law)

locked_facts.joule_heating: Heat produced is proportional to I², R, and time t
locked_facts.i_squared_law: Doubling current quadruples power — P scales as I², not I
locked_facts.series_rule: Same current through all — higher R dissipates MORE power
locked_facts.parallel_rule: Same voltage across all — lower R dissipates MORE power
locked_facts.microscopic_cause: Electrons collide with lattice ions — kinetic energy converts to thermal energy

ncert_truth_statements: P = I²R means doubling current quadruples power, not doubles it
Electrons drift slowly under the electric field while colliding with lattice ions — collisions release heat
Power dissipated in a resistor increases as the square of current (P = I²R)
Heat produced H = I²Rt increases with current, resistance, and time
The electric field drives electron drift and the resulting collisions convert electrical energy to thermal energy

common_misconceptions.belief: Doubling current doubles the heat produced
common_misconceptions.correction: Doubling current quadruples heat — H = I²Rt, the I² relationship is non-linear
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show power meter quadrupling when current doubles — make the jump dramatic

common_misconceptions.belief: In series, same resistance means same brightness
common_misconceptions.correction: Different resistances in series carry same current — P = I²R so higher R glows brighter
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Two resistors side by side, same current, visibly different glow intensity

common_misconceptions.correction: Current is the same before and after the resistor — only energy is lost as heat
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Show current counter unchanged on both sides of resistor while glow increases

simulation_states.STATE_1: No current — wire at room temperature, lattice ions stationary, no glow
simulation_states.STATE_2: Current ON — electrons drifting, collisions begin, resistor starts warming, faint orange glow
simulation_states.STATE_3: Current doubled — glow is 4x brighter NOT 2x, power meter shows I² relationship dramatically
simulation_states.STATE_4: Student controls current slider — watches glow quadruple in real time, H = I²Rt counter rising

aha_moment: STATE_3 is the climax — the glow jumps to 4x when current only doubles. Students expect linear. The quadratic jump is the surprise that makes P = I²R unforgettable.

renderer_hint.zoom_shows: electron-lattice collisions causing vibration and heat glow
renderer_hint.macroscopic_shows: resistor connected to circuit, glowing orange-red with heat

visual_drama.primary_colour: resistor glows deep orange at low current, white-hot at high current
visual_drama.secondary_colour: electrons are blue, lattice ions pulse yellow-white on each collision
visual_drama.scale_change: at STATE_3, glow radius doubles and pulses — visually undeniable quadrupling
visual_drama.surprise_moment: when student drags current from 1A to 2A — power meter jumps from 4W to 16W instantly, not to 8W as expected
visual_drama.zoom_in: realistic resistor on left, microscopic collision animation zoomed in on right panel

legend.meaning: Electric power — rate of energy dissipation (W)
Power scales with square of current
